reason: Both produce a clear primitive humanoid on a visible ground plane with a walk-implied pose and clean metrics. Three.js wins on visual polish: a richer environment (sky, green ground, brown path with footprint decals, grid) plus facial features and colored extremities, with no rendering artifacts. Aura3D's figure is solid and its split-leg stride reads as walking more strongly, but the scene is dark and sparse, the figure faces away (no face), and a translucent box artifact near the right arm looks like a glitch.
auraFailures: Translucent/overlapping box artifact around the right arm reads as a rendering glitch
Figure shown from behind with no facial features; sparse dark environment reduces visual appeal
threeFailures: Walk pose is subtle (legs only slightly apart) and arms hang stiffly, so animation is less obvious in the still
